channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What to watch for in Monday's NCAA championship game
item.description: TNT Sports College Basketball journalist Adam Lefkoe talks about what's at stake in the NCAA Men's National Championship game on Monday night.
item.content:encoded: TNT Sports College Basketball journalist Adam Lefkoe talks about what's at stake in the NCAA Men's National Championship game on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Pope Leo appeals for peace in Easter address
item.description: Pope Leo XIV celebrated his first Easter as pope on Sunday in Rome, and he called for world leaders involved in global conflicts to lay down their arms.
item.content:encoded: Pope Leo XIV celebrated his first Easter as pope on Sunday in Rome, and he called for world leaders involved in global conflicts to lay down their arms.
item.dc:creator: Claire Giangrave

item.title: Show me the money: NPR staff share their favorite movies about money and the economy
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma and Wailin Wong, host of The Indicator podcast, join Rob Schmitz to discuss their favorite serious and less serious movies about money and the economy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma and Wailin Wong, host of The Indicator podcast, join Rob Schmitz to discuss their favorite serious and less serious movies about money and the economy.
item.dc:creator: Adam Raney

item.title: Amid a high-profile scandal, Germany considers deepfake porn punishments
item.description: Congress passed the Take It Down Act in 2024, protecting victims of deepfake revenge pornography. Now, Germany is considering punishing the creators of deepfake porn, not just the distributors, for up to 2 years. NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Harvard Law Professor Rebecca Tushnet.
item.content:encoded: Congress passed the Take It Down Act in 2024, protecting victims of deepfake revenge pornography. Now, Germany is considering punishing the creators of deepfake porn, not just the distributors, for up to 2 years. NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Harvard Law Professor Rebecca Tushnet.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Will Hungary's far-right leader Viktor Orban be voted out of power?
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Zsuzsanna Vegh, program officer at the German Marshall Fund of the United States, about what's at stake in the upcoming election in Hungary.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Zsuzsanna Vegh, program officer at the German Marshall Fund of the United States, about what's at stake in the upcoming election in Hungary.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: Rep. Madeleine Dean discusses Iran and President Trump's proposed budget
item.description: Rep. Madeleine Dean talks about the rescue of a downed U.S. crewmember in Iran and reacts to the president's request for unprecedented defense spending in his proposed budget.
item.content:encoded: Rep. Madeleine Dean talks about the rescue of a downed U.S. crewmember in Iran and reacts to the president's request for unprecedented defense spending in his proposed budget.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: US airman rescued after his fighter jet was shot down in Iran
item.description: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Tom Bowman reports on the race to rescue a U.S. airman after his fighter jet was shot down.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bowman

item.title: Local culture shapes the Zairean liturgy
item.description: Easter in the Democratic Republic of the Congo—where congregants celebrate with the Zairean Rite, the only Vatican-approved liturgy shaped by local culture, alive with song, dance, and striking ritual.
item.content:encoded: Easter in the Democratic Republic of the Congo—where congregants celebrate with the Zairean Rite, the only Vatican-approved liturgy shaped by local culture, alive with song, dance, and striking ritual.
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: German researchers set right the story of a 9,000-year-old shaman's grave
item.description: When a 9,000 year-old grave of a shaman was discovered in Nazi Germany, the discovery was quickly politicized to support Nazi propaganda. But new analysis shows that initial narrative was all wrong.
item.content:encoded: When a 9,000 year-old grave of a shaman was discovered in Nazi Germany, the discovery was quickly politicized to support Nazi propaganda. But new analysis shows that initial narrative was all wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Coach 'Mo' led her team to the DIII women's basketball championship, while fighting cancer
item.description: Denison University's women's basketball team won its first national championship this season, under the leadership of Coach Maureen "Mo" Hirt - who recently celebrated her own victory over Hodgkin's lymphoma.
item.content:encoded: Denison University's women's basketball team won its first national championship this season, under the leadership of Coach Maureen "Mo" Hirt - who recently celebrated her own victory over Hodgkin's lymphoma.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller
